title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
The following afternoon, Anthony arrives by train and meets Inspector Batters, who claims to be traveling to London. Anthony questions Batters' motives and discusses his own failed hunches regarding the burglary at Chimney House. Anthony expresses suspicion toward a French au pair, noting her room light was on during the incident, and mentions a suspicious Frenchman named Chere, a silk salesman staying at the Happy Cricket Inn, whom Anthony believes is connected to the French governess at the villa.

Batters warns Anthony against judging by appearances and mentions that Scotland Yard is searching for Victor King, a world-famous jewel thief and master of disguise. Their conversation shifts to a news report regarding the unidentified body of a foreign man found shot by a roadside, which Batters notes was clearly not a suicide. Anthony feels the Inspector is watching him closely.

While walking toward the mansion, Anthony re-evaluates his observations from the night of the burglary. He realizes his spatial orientation was uncertain and determines the lit window he saw might have been the third room from the end rather than the second. He asks the servant, Trudwell, who occupies that room and learns it belongs to an American guest named Mr. Fitch. Trudwell also confirms that a failed robbery occurred the previous night involving an attempt to disassemble armor statues in the council chamber. Anthony discusses the strange target of the theft with Mr. Isakov, who remarks that the attempt seemed amateurish.
